text.heading: STRIKE BULLETIN # 11
text.p: 1. The Technical Committee (TC) of the Dialogue Committee finished its work on Wednesday, May 7 2003, and produced a signed report, inspite of the series of obstacles placed in its way.

2. The report of the Technical Committee was scheduled for presentation to its principal, the Dialogue Committee yesterday, Thursday, May 8 2003. unfortunately the Minister of Education did not show up. The meeting, therefore, could not discuss the report and was adjourned.
3. The Honourable Minister of Labour has appealed to the union to be patient as he is making effort to reconvene the meeting of the Dialogue Committee next week to discuss the report.
4. Our union remains committed to the resolution of the crisis through the ongoing Dialogue and would attend any re-scheduled meeting, accordingly.
5. National Strike Coordinating Committee (NSCC) is aware of the announcements of resumption in some universities.
6. We salute the resoluteness of our members in the affected branches and urge members in other branches not to be deceived by such announcements. The struggle continues.

7. Government has obviously become more desperate-a lot of people have been and are being massively recruited to play various roles, including putting our loyal members under pressure and blackmail to break the strike. With our collective resolve they will continue to fail, for victory belongs to us and the Nigerian people.
8. Colleagues, our struggle has reached a critical stage and we must remain resolute.

1. Demand from your Chairman a copy of the full briefing on the struggle so far and digest same for your information. Also, get a signed copy of the report of the Technical Committee from your Chairman.
2. Do not sign any register or resumption form in whatever format presented by your university administration.
3. The strike remains total: do not attend any statutory or other meetings, however disguised. Do not teach but continue to do your research.

4. Attend all congress meetings for first-hand information from your Chairman. Worse attempts will be made in the coming days at propaganda and disinformation by Government and its agents. Don't be deceived.
5. Be patient with our detractors; do your best to educate them on the issues at stake. Continue to educate the general public.
Colleagues the stakes are high in this struggle.
REMAIN RESOLUTE! PERSEVERE!! THIS UNION MAKES US STRONG!!!
